reference_groups.entry_label: Origin and cause of the name
reference_groups.raw_segment: Origin and cause of the name —  Ge 2:23.
Originally made

reference_groups.entry_label: By God in his own image.

From one of Adam’s ribs. — Ge 2:21,22.
For man. — 1Co 11:9.

reference_groups.entry_label: To be an helpmeet for man.
reference_groups.raw_segment: To be an helpmeet for man. — Ge 2:18,20.
Subordinate to man. — 1Co 11:3.

reference_groups.entry_label: To be the glory of man.
reference_groups.raw_segment: To be the glory of man. — 1Co 11:7.
Deceived by Satan —  Ge 3:1-6; 2Co 11:3; 1Ti 2:14.
Led man to disobey God —  Ge 3:6,11,12.
Curse pronounced on —  Ge 3:16.

reference_groups.entry_label: Salvation promised through the seed of
reference_groups.raw_segment: Salvation promised through the seed of —  Ge 3:15; Isa 7:14.

reference_groups.entry_label: Safety in childbirth promised to the faithful and holy
reference_groups.raw_segment: Safety in childbirth promised to the faithful and holy —  1Ti 2:15.
Characterised as
Weaker than man. — 1Pe 3:7.
Loving and affectionate. — 2Sa 1:26.

reference_groups.entry_label: Tender and constant to her offspring.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Tender and constant to her offspring. — Isa 49:15; La 4:10.

reference_groups.entry_label: To wear her hair long as a covering
reference_groups.raw_segment: To wear her hair long as a covering —  1Co 11:15.
Good and virtuous, described —  Pr 31:10-28.
Virtuous, held in high estimation —  Ru 3:11; Pr 31:10,30.
Frequently
Fond of self-indulgence. — Isa 32:9-11.
Subtle and deceitful. — Pr 7:10; Ec 7:26.

reference_groups.entry_label: Silly and easily led into error.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Silly and easily led into error. — 2Ti 3:6.

reference_groups.entry_label: Zealous in promoting superstition and idolatry.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Zealous in promoting superstition and idolatry. — Jer 7:18; Eze 13:17,23.
Active in instigating to iniquity. — Nu 31:15,16; 1Ki 21:25; Ne 13:26.

reference_groups.entry_label: Generally wore a vail in the presence of the other sex
reference_groups.raw_segment: Generally wore a vail in the presence of the other sex —  Ge 24:65.

reference_groups.entry_label: Generally lived in a separated apartment or tent
reference_groups.raw_segment: Generally lived in a separated apartment or tent —  Ge 18:9; 24:67; Es 2:9,11.
Submissive and respectful to husbands —  1Pe 3:6; Ge 18:12.
Of distinction
Fair and graceful. — Ge 12:11; 24:16; Song 1:8; Am 8:13.
Haughty in their deportment. — Isa 3:16.
Fond of dress and ornaments. — Isa 3:17-23.

reference_groups.entry_label: Wore their hair plaited and adorned with gold and pearls.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Wore their hair plaited and adorned with gold and pearls. — Isa 3:24; 1Ti 2:9.

reference_groups.entry_label: Of the poorer classes swarthy from exposure to the sun
reference_groups.raw_segment: Of the poorer classes swarthy from exposure to the sun —  Song 1:5,6.
Young
Called maids. — Ex 2:8; Lu 8:51,52.
Called damsels. — Ge 24:55; Mr 5:39.
Called virgins. — Ge 24:16; La 1:4.
Gay and merry. — Jdj 11:34; 21:21; Jer 31:13; Zec 9:17.
Kind and courteous to strangers. — Ge 24:17.
Fond of ornaments. — Jer 2:32.

reference_groups.entry_label: Required to learn from and imitate their elders.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Required to learn from and imitate their elders. — Tit 2:4.

reference_groups.entry_label: Inherited parents’ property when there was no male heir.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Inherited parents’ property when there was no male heir. — Nu 27:8.

reference_groups.entry_label: Could not marry without consent of parents.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Could not marry without consent of parents. — Ge 24:3,4; 34:6; Ex 22:17.

reference_groups.entry_label: Not to be given in marriage considered a calamity.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Not to be given in marriage considered a calamity. — Jdj 11:37; Ps 78:63; Isa 4:1.
Often taken captive. — La 1:18; Eze 30:17,18.
Punishment for seducing, when betrothed. — De 22:23-27.

reference_groups.entry_label: Punishment for seducing when not betrothed.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Punishment for seducing when not betrothed. — Ex 22:16,17; De 22:28,29.

reference_groups.entry_label: Often treated with great cruelty in war.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Often treated with great cruelty in war. — De 32:25; La 2:21; 5:11.

reference_groups.entry_label: Of distinction, dressed in robes of various colours.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Of distinction, dressed in robes of various colours. — 2Sa 13:18; Ps 45:14.

reference_groups.entry_label: Were required to hear and obey the law
reference_groups.raw_segment: Were required to hear and obey the law —  Jos 8:35.

reference_groups.entry_label: Had a court of the tabernacle assigned to them
reference_groups.raw_segment: Had a court of the tabernacle assigned to them —  Ex 38:8; 1Sa 2:2.

reference_groups.entry_label: Allowed to join in the temple-music from the time of David
reference_groups.raw_segment: Allowed to join in the temple-music from the time of David —  1Ch 25:5,6; Ezr 2:65; Ne 7:67.
Often engaged in
Domestic employments. — Ge 18:6; Pr 31:15.
Agriculture. — Ru 2:8; Song 1:6.
Tending sheep. — Ge 29:9; Ex 2:16.
Drawing and carrying water. — Ge 24:11,13,15,16; 1Sa 9:11; Joh 4:7.
Grinding corn. — Mt 24:41; Lu 17:35.
Spinning. — Pr 31:13,14.
Embroidery. — Pr 31:22.

reference_groups.entry_label: Celebrating the victories of the nation.
reference_groups.raw_segment: Celebrating the victories of the nation. — Ex 15:20,21; Jdj 11:34; 1Sa 18:6,7.
Attending funerals as mourners. — Jer 9:17,20.

reference_groups.entry_label: Vows of, when married not binding upon the husband
reference_groups.raw_segment: Vows of, when married not binding upon the husband —  Nu 30:6-8.

reference_groups.entry_label: Unfaithfulness of, when married found out by the waters of jealousy
reference_groups.raw_segment: Unfaithfulness of, when married found out by the waters of jealousy —  Nu 5:14-28.

reference_groups.entry_label: Punishment for injuring, when with child
reference_groups.raw_segment: Punishment for injuring, when with child —  Ex 21:22-25.

reference_groups.entry_label: To be governed by, considered a calamity by the Jews
reference_groups.raw_segment: To be governed by, considered a calamity by the Jews —  Isa 3:12.

reference_groups.entry_label: To be slain by, considered a great disgrace
reference_groups.raw_segment: To be slain by, considered a great disgrace —  Jdj 9:54.

reference_groups.entry_label: Considered a valuable booty in war
reference_groups.raw_segment: Considered a valuable booty in war —  De 20:14; 1Sa 30:2.

reference_groups.entry_label: Often treated with great cruelty in war
reference_groups.raw_segment: Often treated with great cruelty in war —  2Ki 8:12; La 5:11; Eze 9:6; Ho 13:16.
Illustrative

reference_groups.entry_label: (Gloriously arrayed,) of the Church of Christ.
reference_groups.raw_segment: (Gloriously arrayed,) of the Church of Christ. — Ps 45:13; Ga 4:26; Re 12:1.
(Delicate,) of backsliding Israel. — Jer 6:2.
(Chaste and holy,) of saints. — Song 1:3; 2Co 11:2; Re 14:4.
(Lewd,) of the Roman apostasy. — Re 17:4,18.
(Wise,) of saints. — Mt 25:1,2,4.
(Foolish,) of mere professors. — Mt 25:1-3.

reference_groups.entry_label: (At ease and careless,) of a state of carnal security.
reference_groups.raw_segment: (At ease and careless,) of a state of carnal security. — Isa 32:9,11.
(Forsaken,) of Israel in her captivity. — Isa 54:6.

definitions.text: Was “taken out of man” (Gen. 2:23), and therefore the man has the preeminence. “The head of the woman is the man;” but yet honour is to be shown to the wife, “as unto the weaker vessel” (1 Cor. 11:3, 8, 9; 1 Pet. 3:7). Several women are mentioned in Scripture as having been endowed with prophetic gifts, as Miriam (Ex. 15:20), Deborah (Judg. 4:4, 5), Huldah (2 Kings 22:14), Noadiah (Neh. 6:14), Anna (Luke 2:36, 37), and the daughters of Philip the evangelist (Acts 21:8, 9). Women are forbidden to teach publicly (1 Cor. 14:34, 35; 1 Tim. 2:11, 12). Among the Hebrews it devolved upon women to prepare the meals for the household (Gen. 18:6; 2 Sam. 13:8), to attend to the work of spinning (Ex. 35:26; Prov. 31:19), and making clothes (1 Sam. 2:19; Prov. 31:21), to bring water from the well (Gen. 24:15; 1 Sam. 9:11), and to care for the flocks (Gen. 29:6; Ex. 2:16). The word “woman,” as used in Matt. 15:28, John 2:4 and 20:13, 15, implies tenderness and courtesy and not disrespect. Only where revelation is known has woman her due place of honour assigned to her.
